summary: Machine policy for whether Windows connects to the OneSettings service to download configuration settings. Microsoft documents the same device policy control and registry key path that the app writes, and the local ADMX confirms the value mapping.

setting.name: OneSettings Downloads
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can periodically connect to the OneSettings service to download configuration settings. This policy decides whether those download attempts happen.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable OneSettings downloads to reduce background contact with Microsoft services or to lock down external configuration retrieval on managed systems.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it reduces OneSettings connectivity, but it can also stop Microsoft-delivered configuration settings that rely on that service.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisableOneSettingsDownloads in the System Policy CSP and DataCollection.admx maps it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableOneSettingsDownloads with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Allow OneSettings downloads
allowed_values.meaning: Windows can periodically attempt to connect to the OneSettings service to download configuration settings.

allowed_values.label: Disable OneSettings downloads
allowed_values.meaning: Windows will not attempt to connect to the OneSettings service.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableOneSettingsDownloads = 1, which matches the documented state that blocks OneSettings connectivity.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 11 version 21H2 and later systems exposing the policy

states.rationale: The policy is enabled only when administrators explicitly turn downloads off.

windows_defaults.notes: By default, Windows periodically attempts to connect to the OneSettings service.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that rely on standard Microsoft configuration retrieval
avoid_for: Systems that should minimize Microsoft service connectivity

states.rationale: Keep the default service connectivity behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal OneSettings download behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to prevent those background configuration downloads.
tradeoffs: Windows can retrieve configuration settings from the OneSettings service.
Background contact with that Microsoft service remains allowed.

recommended_profiles.label: Reduced OneSettings connectivity
intended_for: Managed environments with tighter outbound policy
avoid_for: Systems that expect Microsoft configuration downloads through OneSettings

states.rationale: Prevent Windows from attempting to connect to the OneSettings service.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to minimize this Microsoft service connection path.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to keep downloading configuration settings from OneSettings.
tradeoffs: OneSettings connectivity is blocked by policy.
Related Microsoft-provided configuration downloads can stop.

evidence.title: Microsoft System Policy CSP: DisableOneSettingsDownloads
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the behavior and policy mapping for DisableOneSettingsDownloads, including that enabling the policy stops Windows from attempting to connect to the OneSettings service.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableOneSettingsDownloads with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy stops Windows from attempting to connect to the OneSettings service, while disabled or not configured allows periodic connection attempts.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableOneSettingsDownloads = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableOneSettingsDownloads policy
evidence.summary: DataCollection.admx shows DisableOneSettingsDownloads under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy stops Windows from attempting to connect with the OneSettings service, while disabling or not configuring it allows periodic OneSettings downloads. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents DisableOneSettingsDownloads directly in the System Policy CSP, including the exact control name, behavior, allowed values, and registry key path under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection. The page doesn't show a separate Registry Value Name row, but the local ADMX confirms the value name matches the control name, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy setting controls whether Windows attempts to connect with the OneSettings service. If you enable this policy, Windows won't attempt to connect with the OneSettings Service. ... Default Value 0. Allowed values: 0 (Default) Not disabled. 1 Disabled. Group policy mapping: Name DisableOneSettingsDownloads ... Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection.
validation_proof.notes: The System Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact control and registry key path used by the app. The separate Registry Value Name row isn't shown on the page, so the record relies on the local DataCollection.admx file to confirm that the value name is also DisableOneSettingsDownloads. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-disableonesettingsdownloads-admx.
